channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Kuria Denies Violence Accusations, Warns Gachagua's Political Strategy Will Fail
item.description: Senior government official Moses Kuria has dismissed allegations by former deputy president Rigathi Gachagua that he planned violence during a rally in Ol Kalou. In a statement, Kuria called the accus...

item.title: Tanzania advocates for international guidelines on ethical AI use
item.description: The article highlights Tanzania's initiative to push for global regulations ensuring artificial intelligence is developed and deployed responsibly. While the main headline focuses on AI governance, th...

item.title: Three Suspects Get Life Imprisonment for Oriire Abduction Case
item.description: The Federal High Court in Abuja has sentenced three suspects to life imprisonment for their involvement in the abduction of students and teachers in Oriire Local Government Area of Oyo State. Abdulraz...

item.title: Senator Darline Graham Nordone Faces Backlash for Donating to Pro-Abortion Candidate
item.description: U.S. Senator Darline Graham Nordone (R-SC), the appointed replacement approved by former President Donald Trump, has faced intense criticism from MAGA supporters for donating $100 to state Sen. Katrin...

item.title: Pakistan's Foreign Policy Challenges at Asean Regional Forum
item.description: The article details Pakistan's participation in the Asean Regional Forum (ARF) where Foreign Minister Shah Mahmood Quraishi outlined critical challenges facing the country and its neighbors. Key issue...

item.title: Kesha Makes Surprise Appearance at St. John’s Hotel Bar
item.description: Kesha, the American singer known for her hit songs, made an unexpected appearance at a hotel bar in St. John’s, Newfoundland, sparking excitement among patrons. The event, which occurred on July 23, 2...

item.title: Teenage Fanclub announce 13th album ‘Do Not Dare To Dream’ and share new single ‘Day In The Sun’
item.description: Scottish indie veterans Teenage Fanclub have announced their upcoming 13th studio album, ‘Do Not Dare To Dream’, alongside the release of a new single titled ‘Day In The Sun’. The album will arrive on...

item.title: Johannesburg's Marble Towers Closure Displaces Hundreds of Immigrant Traders
item.description: The City of Johannesburg's demolition of illegal structures at the Marble Towers precinct has displaced hundreds of immigrant traders, primarily Ethiopian and Congolese, who lost their livelihoods. Th...

item.title: BJP MP Nishikant Dubey Advises Students to Prioritize Studies Over Protests
item.description: BJP MP Nishikant Dubey addressed protesting students at Jantar Mantar, urging them to focus on education rather than participating in demonstrations over the NEET paper leak controversy. He emphasized...

item.title: Tolosa retains dual roles in Sara Duterte impeachment trial despite advice
item.description: Benjamin Tolosa, Jr., the legal spokesperson for the House impeachment prosecution panel against Vice President Sara Duterte, confirmed he will continue holding both his role as legal spokesperson and...

item.title: Haim Yavin, Pioneer of Israeli Television, Passes Away at 93
item.description: Haim Yavin, widely regarded as the father of Israeli television news, has passed away at the age of 93. The veteran broadcaster anchored Israel's first TV news bulletin in 1968 and remained a key figu...

item.title: India offers 1,135 scholarships for Tanzanian students
item.description: India has launched a scholarship program offering 1,135 opportunities for Tanzanian students to pursue higher education. The initiative aims to strengthen bilateral ties between the two nations by sup...

item.title: Police raid leads to deaths of suspects in JPMC doctor murder case
item.description: The Special Investigation Unit (SIU) confirmed that three suspects arrested in connection with the killing of Dr. Akash Kumar, a doctor at Jinnah Postgraduate Medical Centre (JPMC), died in an exchang...

item.title: Iran drone attacks on CIA sites spark US intelligence probe into possible Russian support
item.description: Iranian drones have targeted CIA facilities in the Gulf region, prompting US intelligence agencies to investigate whether Russia provided advanced technology or targeting data for the strikes. Reports...

item.title: Millionaires Push for Higher Taxes on Wealthiest in UK
item.description: A group of 120 wealthy individuals, including former footballer Gary Lineker and music producer Brian Eno, have written an open letter to new Prime Minister Andy Burnham urging a higher tax on the ric...

item.title: Kogi State Compensates Victims of Demolition, Advances Airport Projects
item.description: The Kogi State Government has compensated affected residents after demolishing a structure used by criminals in Idojie-Okene, Okene Local Government Area. Governor Ahmed Usman Ododo, represented by Di...

item.title: Wafcon 2026 Tournament Details and Prize Money Breakdown
item.description: The 2026 Women’s Africa Cup of Nations (Wafcon) is set to kick off in Morocco on July 16, 2026, with 16 teams competing for a total prize pool of R32m. This marks a 100% increase from previous edition...

item.title: South Africa Explains Emergency Hysterectomy Protocols and Patient Rights
item.description: The article discusses emergency hysterectomies as life-saving procedures during childbirth when uncontrollable bleeding occurs. It clarifies that such surgeries are only performed when all other measu...

item.title: US-Saudi Nuclear Deal Conditional on Riyadh Joining Abraham Accords
item.description: US President Donald Trump announced that a landmark nuclear cooperation agreement with Saudi Arabia is contingent on Riyadh joining the Abraham Accords and normalizing relations with Israel. The deal,...

item.title: President Ruto Announces KSh 80 Million for Nairobi Artisans and Mechanics to Upgrade Equipment
item.description: President William Ruto has pledged KSh 80 million to support Nairobi's informal sector artisans and mechanics by providing modern tools and equipment. Over 5,000 workers attended a meeting at State Ho...
